title: Efficient Non-Base-Station based Wireless Communication in MANET using Varying Parameters
author: K.Thamizhmaran
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A beacon packet is a little packet that is transmitted to announce the presence of a base station. The mobile units detect the beacons and try to connect wirelessly. Geographic routing methods, which base their forwarding decisions only on the local topology, have garnered a lot of attention lately.    The limited resources of MANET nodes, such as multi-path communication, end-to-end latency, routing conjunction, residual energy, bandwidth, and storage, set them apart.     Due to topology, MANET routing is a significant problem. Using Network Simulator 2 (NS2), this new research article was created using my previously published paper and suggested multipath work with three different topology sizes, three different node sets, and three different malicious node sets. It includes a number of parameters, including packet delivery ratio, throughput, routing overhead, packet loss, delay, and remaining energy.

keywords: MANET, Attack, Energy, Routing Protocols, Network Simulator
